text.title: RAG Feature Acceptance Contract — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that must hold for any RAG feature acceptance contract. These are the HARD rules — stakeholder pressure to ship does not override them.

rule.statement: A single contract MUST decompose the RAG feature into ≥2 named intent classes (e.g. policy-lookup, troubleshooting, comparison). One global metric set across all intents is forbidden.

rationale.source: Pinecone — Why your RAG fails in production (2024); Anthropic — Building effective agents (2024)

rule.rationale: Different intents tolerate different failure modes. A policy lookup MUST be faithful (no hallucination); a brainstorming intent MUST be relevant but may diverge from sources. Averaging metrics across intents hides class-specific failures and ships features that are correct on average but broken on the intent that actually matters to revenue.

rule.statement: The contract is invalid until three roles have explicitly signed: Product Manager (user outcome + acceptable failure mode), Subject-Matter Expert (correctness rubric with examples), ML engineer (offline metric thresholds + online metric pair). No proxy signatures.

rationale.source: Google PAIR — People + AI Guidebook; Microsoft Responsible AI Standard v2

rule.rationale: Without PM, thresholds disconnect from business outcome. Without SME, "correct" defaults to LLM-judge consensus which drifts toward fluent-but-wrong. Without ML engineer, thresholds are aspirational, not measurable. All three signatures must be in version control, dated, with names.

rule.statement: Every offline metric threshold in the contract MUST be paired with at least one online metric. Offline-only contracts are forbidden because offline metrics on a fixed eval set predict shipped quality poorly.

rationale.source: Shankar et al. — Who validates the validators? (2024); industry post-mortems from Bing, Notion AI

rule.rationale: Eval-set overfit is the dominant RAG failure pattern. Offline faithfulness ≥ 0.85 on a 200-query golden set does not predict whether real users escalate to humans. The contract MUST list the production telemetry signal (CSAT, deflection rate, thumbs-down rate, follow-up-query rate) that confirms the offline gate held in reality.

rule.statement: Every numerical threshold MUST cite either (a) the baseline number it improves over (current system, human baseline, or competitor), or (b) the user-acceptable error rate from PM. "0.85 because it sounds high" is invalid.

rationale.source: DORA — Accelerate (measure what matters); classical industrial QC

rule.rationale: A threshold without a baseline is unfalsifiable. The contract MUST capture: "Current human-handled deflection is X%. We accept shipping at ≥0.7X% deflection because SME judges the answer quality at that point matches a junior agent." This makes the bar negotiable in writing.

rule.statement: The contract MUST define explicit recontract triggers: query-distribution drift threshold, model upgrade event, SME availability for re-rubric, or detected online-vs-offline divergence ≥ 20%. When triggered, the contract is opened, not silently revised.

rationale.source: Anthropic — Evals (2024); production ML drift literature (Tabular, EvidentlyAI)

rule.rationale: Without a recontract trigger, the contract becomes wallpaper. The most common production failure is the silent drift: query mix shifts, embeddings model is updated, the offline eval set still passes, but online metrics fall. The recontract trigger turns drift into a calendar event with named owners.

example.title: Two-intent contract excerpt
example.code: feature: support-rag intents: policy-lookup: pm_outcome: "Resolve customer policy questions without human escalation" acceptable_failure: "Refuse to answer (route to human) is acceptable; hallucinated policy text is NOT" sme_rubric: pass: "Cites correct policy section; quoted text matches source verbatim" borderline: "Cites correct section; paraphrases instead of quoting" fail: "No citation OR cited section does not contain the claim" offline: faithfulness: ">= 0.92" # baseline: junior agent 0.94 context-precision: ">= 0.85" online: thumbs-down-rate: "<= 3%" # baseline: today 7% escalation-rate: "<= 12%" # baseline: today 22% troubleshooting: pm_outcome: "Walk user through fix; partial diagnosis is acceptable" acceptable_failure: "Wrong-but-safe step is acceptable; destructive wrong step is NOT" sme_rubric: pass: "Steps in correct order; no destructive step before confirmation" fail: "Recommends data-destructive action without prior backup step" offline: answer-relevance: ">= 0.80" destructive-step-detector: "== 0" # hard zero online: follow-up-rephrase-rate: "<= 18%" data-loss-incidents: "== 0" signatures: pm: "alice@co 2026-04-12 v1.2" sme: "ravi@co 2026-04-12 v1.2" ml_engineer: "jonas@co 2026-04-12 v1.2" recontract_triggers: - "query_intent_distribution_shift_chi2_p < 0.01" - "embedding_model_upgrade" - "online_vs_offline_divergence >= 0.20"
example.note: Illustrates r1 (two intents), r2 (three signatures with dates), r3 (offline+online pairs), r4 (baselines cited), r5 (explicit triggers).

example.title: Single-intent vanity-metric contract
example.code: feature: support-rag metrics: bleu: ">= 0.4" rouge_l: ">= 0.5" signed_off_by: "team lead"
example.note: r1 violation (no intents), r2 violation (no PM / SME / ML triad), r3 violation (no online metric), r4 violation (no baseline; BLEU/ROUGE are notoriously poor proxies for RAG quality), r5 missing.
